Research Tool Only: This application is intended for research and educational purposes. It does not constitute medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment recommendations. Clinical decisions must be made by qualified healthcare professionals using complete patient information. Guideline recommendations shown are summaries and may not reflect the full context, caveats, or patient-specific considerations of the source documents.

Welcome to MetaSprint NMA

This tool guides you through a complete network meta-analysis in a structured 40-day sprint.

  1. Discover -- Explore the RCT landscape and find evidence gaps for treatment comparisons
  2. Protocol -- Define your PICO and register on PROSPERO
  3. Search -- Search cardiology RCT sources (PubMed, OpenAlex, CT.gov, AACT)
  4. Screen -- Screen titles/abstracts with keyboard shortcuts (I=Include, E=Exclude, M=Maybe)
  5. Extract -- Enter treatment comparisons, effect sizes, CIs, and risk of bias
  6. Analyze -- Run network meta-analysis (frequentist or Bayesian) with consistency testing
  7. Write -- Auto-generate Methods and Results sections

The Dashboard tracks your progress across 5 quality gates (Definition of Done). Use the Checkpoints tab to sign off each gate.

Tip: Use the day counter in the header to track your sprint progress. Press ? anytime for help.

Workflow

Follow tabs left to right: Discover -> Protocol -> Search -> Screen -> Extract -> Analyze -> Write. The Dashboard and Checkpoints track your progress.

Network Meta-Analysis Concepts

NMA (Network Meta-Analysis)
Simultaneously compares multiple treatments using both direct and indirect evidence across a network of RCTs.
Frequentist NMA
Graph-theoretic approach (Rucker 2012). Solves Laplacian L = BᵀWB for treatment effects. Fast, deterministic.
Bayesian NMA
Lu & Ades (2004) consistency model with MCMC sampling. Provides posterior distributions, SUCRA, and rank probabilities.
Consistency
The assumption that direct and indirect evidence agree. Tested via node-splitting (Dias 2010) and global Q decomposition.
P-score / SUCRA
Treatment ranking: P-score (frequentist, 0-1) or SUCRA (Bayesian). Higher = better treatment performance.
Contribution matrix
Shows how much each direct comparison contributes to each network estimate (Papakonstantinou 2018).

Effect Types

MD (Mean Difference)
Continuous outcomes, same measurement scale
log(OR), log(RR), log(HR)
Log-transformed ratio measures for binary/time-to-event outcomes
SMD (Standardized Mean Difference)
Continuous outcomes across different scales
RD (Risk Difference)
Absolute difference in binary outcomes

Analysis Interpretation

League table
All-vs-all treatment comparison matrix with effect estimates and 95% CIs. Green = significantly favours row treatment.
Node-splitting
Compares direct vs indirect evidence for each comparison. p < 0.05 flags inconsistency (Dias 2010).
I-squared
<25% Low, 25-50% Moderate, 50-75% Substantial, >75% Considerable heterogeneity
HKSJ
Knapp-Hartung modification: uses t-distribution for CIs (wider, more honest for small k)
R-hat
Gelman-Rubin convergence diagnostic for Bayesian MCMC. <1.05 indicates convergence.

Al-Burhan Analysis Engines

Engine names use Arabic evidence terminology (Al-Burhan = "the proof")

TSA (Trial Sequential Analysis)
Sequential monitoring with O'Brien-Fleming boundaries. Determines if evidence is "firm" (benefit), "harmful" (stop for harm), "insufficient", or "futile"
IJMA ("consensus") — Cross-Condition Borrowing
Empirical Bayes hierarchical shrinkage across drug classes for the same outcome
GHAYB ("the unseen") — Ghost Protocol Sensitivity
Publication bias tipping point analysis using registered-but-unpublished trials
TAWATUR ("continuous transmission") — Evidence Velocity
Cumulative meta-analysis trajectory showing how evidence evolves over time
HIJRA ("migration") — Transportability
Population representativeness scoring against real-world epidemiology baselines

FURQAN Discovery Classification

CONFIRMED
Our pooled estimate agrees with published meta-analyses
UPDATED
Same direction as published, but magnitude has shifted with new evidence
CONTRADICTED
Our estimate contradicts published meta-analyses (opposite direction)
NOVEL
No published meta-analysis exists for this comparison
GHOST
Ghost protocols detected: registered trials that never published results

SHAHID Validation

SHAHID compares Al-Burhan pooled estimates against published Cochrane systematic reviews to validate accuracy. "Concordant" means the estimate agrees, "discordant" means it differs.

Definition of Done (DoD) Checkpoints

Track completion of each quality gate. All items must be checked before a gate can be signed off.

Scoping phase (PRISMA 2020): Explore the RCT landscape to identify gaps and formulate your review question. Define your PICO in the Protocol tab before proceeding to formal Search. Discovery findings should inform — not determine — your research question.
Pick a subspecialty for fast loading (~10-30 sec)

Landscape Analytics

PROSPERO Protocol

Define your review question using PICO (Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes). The generated protocol can be submitted to PROSPERO.

Search Strategy

Cardio-only sources: PubMed, OpenAlex, CT.gov + optional AACT PostgreSQL (run python aact_proxy.py for local AACT)

Extracts HR/OR/RR/MD from CT.gov results sections and PubMed structured abstracts. Every number shown with source evidence for verification.

Abstract Screening

0 references

CardioRCT Auto-Screener

Universe-aware screening with deterministic safety gates, calibrated probability model, and recall-first guardrails. Hard-gates use trial registry matching; soft scoring uses BM25 + PICO + Pillar + RCT/Cardio signals.

Auto-Include: 0 Auto-Exclude: 0 Needs Review: 0

Select a reference to view details

PRISMA Flow

Data Extraction

Enter one row per pairwise comparison: Treatment 1 vs Treatment 2, with effect estimate and CI. Multi-arm trials: enter all pairwise comparisons sharing the same Study ID.

Note: Confidence intervals are assumed to be 95%. Adjust in Analysis Settings if your studies report different CI levels.

Data extraction table for included studies
Study IDTrial ID *NCTPMIDDOI Outcome *Timepoint *PopulationVerify Treatment 1 * Treatment 2 * N1 N2 Effect * Lower CI * Upper CI * SE Type Subgroup Notes

Risk of Bias (RoB 2) ▼

Assess 5 bias domains per study: Randomization, Deviations, Missing Data, Measurement, Selection. Results auto-feed into GRADE assessment.

Network Meta-Analysis Dashboard

WebR: Validate with R in Your Browser

Not loaded
Runs netmeta directly in your browser via WebAssembly. Compares tau², I², treatment effects, and P-scores against the app. First load: ~30-60s (cached after). No data leaves your browser.
Initializing WebR...
R
Click "Load R & Validate" after running NMA to verify results against R netmeta
Alternative: Use "R Validation Script" in the export bar to download a standalone .R file for local R/RStudio.
▶

Canonical R Validation (5 Scenarios)

Not run
Runs the app's NMA engine on 5 canonical datasets and cross-validates every metric against R netmeta via WebR. Tests: tau² (DL/REML/PM), treatment effects, P-scores, league table, prediction intervals, HKSJ adjustment, node-splitting, Q decomposition, and contribution matrix. First run: ~60-90s (loads R + netmeta). No data leaves your browser.
Initializing...
5 Scenarios
Click "Run All Scenarios" to cross-validate the NMA engine against R netmeta on known datasets
Bayesian (gemtc): Pre-computed reference values embedded; no MCMC runs in browser.

Paper Generator

Auto-generate Methods and Results sections from your review data.

Evidence Insights

Deep evidence analysis inspired by Quranic epistemological principles. Select a sub-tab to explore.

Tawakkul — Trust Decomposition

8-axis radar showing trustworthiness dimensions of the current evidence base. Each axis maps to a GRADE or methodological quality domain.

Run an analysis first (Analyze tab) to see trust decomposition.

Radar chart showing 8 trustworthiness dimensions of the evidence base including risk of bias, inconsistency, imprecision, indirectness, publication bias, dose-response, large effect, and plausible confounding.

Mizan — Harm-Benefit Equipoise

Balance-scale visualization weighing benefits against harms for the pooled intervention.

Extract study data with both efficacy and safety outcomes to see the balance.

Benefit-harm balance chart plotting efficacy against safety outcomes for each treatment in the network.

Shura — Multiverse Specification Curve

How sensitive are results to analytic choices? Runs all permutations of model, estimator, and adjustments.

Need at least 3 studies in the Analyze tab to run multiverse analysis.

Specification curve showing treatment effect estimates under all permutations of model type, heterogeneity estimator, and adjustment method.

Hikmah — Clinical Decision Context

Personalized NNT/NNH calculator. Enter patient baseline risk to see individualized treatment effects.

Run an analysis with binary outcomes (OR/RR/HR) to use the NNT calculator.


Fitrah — Natural Query Interface

Ask questions about your evidence in plain language.

Ihsan — Excellence in Uncertainty Communication

Quantile dot plots and icon arrays that convey uncertainty more intuitively than confidence intervals alone.

Run an analysis first to see uncertainty visualizations.

Quantile Dot Plot

Quantile dot plot visualizing the posterior distribution of the treatment effect as discrete dots representing probability mass.

Icon Array (per 100 patients)

Evidence Accumulation Timeline

Evidence accumulation timeline showing how the pooled treatment effect and confidence interval have evolved as studies were added over time.

Amanah — Evidence Gap Accountability

Structured registry of what evidence is missing. Gaps scored by disease burden, feasibility, and equity impact.

Load trial universe data (Discover tab) to identify evidence gaps.

Taqwa — Integrity Sentinel

Automated checks for statistical integrity: GRIM test (granularity), sample size consistency, and funding bias indicators.

Extract study data first to run integrity checks.

Dhulm — Health Equity Detector

Assesses whether the evidence base represents the populations who bear the disease burden.

Load trial universe or extract study data to assess equity.

Representation equity chart comparing the geographic and demographic distribution of trial participants against the global disease burden.

Rahma — Patient-Facing Translation

Generates plain-language summaries at 8th-grade reading level. Designed for shared decision-making.

Run an analysis first to generate a patient-facing summary.

Tabayyun — Verification Engine

Quran 49:6: "Verify before acting." Every evidence claim gets a cross-source verification state. No insight is trusted unless corroborated by 2+ independent sources.

Load universe data or extract studies to see verification states.

Living Evidence Mode

PRISMA-LSR compliant currency tracking. Shows when evidence was last verified, what changed, and what needs updating.

Load universe data to enable living evidence tracking.

Conflict Detector

Identifies contradictory outcomes across different sources, study updates, or time periods within the evidence base.

Run an analysis or load Al-Burhan data to detect conflicts.

Opportunity Radar — New Since Last MA

Identifies drug-outcome pairs where new trials have emerged since the last published meta-analysis, creating opportunities for updated reviews.

Load universe data to scan for new evidence opportunities.

Registry Graph — Canonical Record Linking

Links NCT IDs, PMIDs, and DOIs into unified canonical records. Detects duplicate registrations and publication-registration mismatches.

Load universe data or extract studies to build the registry graph.

Extract Effect Data

Paste trial results text or upload a PDF. Imports only high-confidence, complete-CI extractions and removes duplicates. Requires RCT Extractor at localhost:8000.
